title: Kendall Gibson
entries: She's very kind and did offer bonus quizzes to help with low quiz scores. However, the lectures were often too vague, making the homework and exams significantly harder than expected. Overall, she was an okay instructor, but final grades were not curved much compared to other professors (like Selin).

She's really nice, however she is a new professor and that is quite obvious because her lectures aren't that great. She tries her best though. You have to take initiative, go to class even though its not mandatory,otherwise this class will be pretty difficult. She doesnt curve the class at the end which is annoying but overall her class was great.

Kendall is just a pleasure to be around -- I always looked forward to attending her office hours. She made the 8 AM lecture bearable even on little sleep. However, she is a harsh grader and will dock you points for little mistakes -- it is super important that you understand the nuts and bolts of math.

I have always struggled in math. This class was great. Professor Gibson is so nice, helpful, and easy to understand. She encourages questions during class and the exam questions are based on given material. The exams are not hard, just take time to study/prepare. Don't be afraid to take this core class, you'll do great!!
Very sweet and caring, explains the topics well. Highly recommend taking her class!

You should know calculus very well before taking her class. Most people end up self-teaching everything. The quizzes each week are annoying, but you'll get through them. The midterm and final are somewhat hard if you're not that good at math. Make sure to review notes and homework before exams.
Do not take her class and I repeat. If u don't care abt curve then take her. Selin has wayyyy better curves she's a sweet person not professor tho.

The lectures are too easy and rushed compared to the difficulty of the hw and exams, I self-studied whole semester and did pretty well on most tests. If u have a solid math background and are comfortable learning the material on your own, this class should be manageable.

The class was very difficult. All of the written homework was much more difficult than what was taught in lecture. There are two midterms and a final—which were all way more difficult than any material we learned from lectures. I think that this is just a hard class, but the professor was easy to find outside of class and nice.

If you need calc for credit don't take her unless you have previously taken AP Calc. Lots of homework. Midterms/ Finals are based on WRITTEN homework which is way harder then what you learn in class. Choose Selin. She is a nice person but not a good professor.

8 am lectures, not mandatory but definitely recommended if you want to actually understand the class. With many opportunities to drop quizzes, problem sets, and midterms, she wants you to pass the class! Approachable when asked about questions, explains until you understand.

I recommend CORE-UA 110 Great Ideas in Mathematics with Prof Gibson! She explains concepts super clearly in lectures, and offers a wide range of times to meet for office hours. She is very kind and genuinely wants you to do well. Not difficult to get an A if you pay attention and apply yourself. Good class for fulfilling core requirement.

If you have previous knowledge about the course you are taking with her, it's not too horrible. However, if you have never taken calculus prior to this class, a lot of topics are brushed over because they assume you know how to do it. There's not a lot of detail in lectures, and I found I learned more in recitation than I did in class.
Very sweet professor. Her lectures make the concepts easy to understand. The exams are hard but hopefully there will be a curve :)

As a Professor she is not bad at all she explains the concepts in class well. However, the content on the exams and homeworks are much more difficult. I would not recommend this class if you are aiming for A's but if you are fine with a B/B+ the class is pretty attainable.

Exams were worth 70% altogether. She's a good professor, but the curve is horrible. Doesn't go much in depth on more of the trickier questions. Homework is manageable, but the lectures are just pretty vague in comparison to how the test questions are. Pray you have a good TA; I couldn't even understand mine.
Choose professor Selin, she curves the average by 10 points. She give the worse curve out of all professors.

Honestly, a pretty good teacher herself, NYU just makes this class feel incredibly rushed (2 new topics every week). The class is hard; the lectures don't go in depth (but are useful). I have learned mostly everything through YouTube. Midterms similar to questions on homework / weekly quizzes, average grades in the 60s. Pray for a good TA
Take this class if you are trying to fail calc
Professor is very nice and pretty good at explaining the concepts. However, the exams are way harder than any of the practice material or lecture questions, averages grades being
